name: Blackened Redfish
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the seasoning mix:
- 1 tablespoon Sweet paprika
- 2 1/2 teaspoons Salt
- 1 teaspoon Onion powder
- 1 teaspoon Garlic powder
- 1 teaspoon Ground cayenne pepper
- 3/4 teaspoon Ground white pepper
- 3/4 teaspoon Ground black pepper
- 1/2 teaspoon Dried thyme leaves
- 1/2 teaspoon Dried oregano leaves
2. Heat a large cast-iron skillet over very high heat until it is beyond the smoking stage and you see white ash in the skillet bottom (at least 10 minutes).
3. Meanwhile, pour 2 tablespoons melted butter into each of 6 small ramekins; keep warm. Reserve the remaining butter in the skillet.
4. Heat serving plates in a 250°F oven.
5. In a small bowl, combine all seasoning mix ingredients.
6. Dip each fillet in the reserved melted butter so that both sides are well coated. Sprinkle the seasoning mix generously and evenly on both sides of the fillets, patting by hand.
7. Place the fillets in the hot skillet and pour 1 teaspoon melted butter on top of each fillet (be careful as the butter may flare up). Cook, uncovered, over the same high heat until the underside looks charred, about 2 minutes (the cooking time will vary according to the fillet's thickness and the skillet's heat).
8. Turn the fish over and pour 1 teaspoon butter on top; cook until the fish is done, about 2 more minutes.
9. Repeat with remaining fillets.
10. Serve each fillet while piping hot, placing one fillet and a ramekin of butter on each heated serving plate.
